summary: Preinstall beacon exfiltrates host identifiers to hardcoded OAST domain and bare IP
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall hook executes index.js, which collects os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, __dirname, and process.cwd(), hex-encodes them into a DNS subdomain query against `*.d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live` (out-of-band DNS exfiltration via a Project Discovery Interactsh-style collaborator), and HTTP-POSTs the same JSON payload to bare IP 172.201.213.59:9090/c. The fetch fires automatically with no TLS and errors are swallowed so the beacon fails silently. The package version (99.0.0) and `@nstrlabs` scope are consistent with a dependency-confusion attack: a high version is published to the public registry to override an internal-scope resolution and harvest hostnames/usernames from internal build environments. Installer harm is automatic at install time and produces a victim list usable for follow-on targeting.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
